response.explanation:
**Continuing Airworthiness Management Responsibilities** The continuing airworthiness management system (CAMO) is responsible for ensuring that all aircraft remain safe and compliant throughout their operational life. The key responsibilities, as outlined in the retrieved material, are grouped into several areas: | Area | What the CAMO Must Do | Who Is Involved | |------|----------------------|-----------------| | **Nominated Postholder & Management Personnel** | • The nominated postholder must have corporate authority to finance and carry out all continuing‑airworthiness activities to the required standard.
• They must ensure that maintenance is performed on time and to an approved standard, and that the organization has the necessary Part‑M authority. | • Nominated postholder (often the same person in small operators)
• Group of persons required by M.A.706(c) (the “continuing‑airworthiness group”) | | **Manpower & Training** | • Provide broad figures showing that enough people are assigned to the CAMO.
• Show a training policy that demonstrates all staff are properly trained for their roles. | • All staff involved in continuing airworthiness (full‑time, part‑time, equivalent full‑time) | | **Records & Documentation** | • Maintain an aircraft continuing‑airworthiness records system and technical log.
• Record flight hours and cycles accurately for maintenance planning. | • CAMO staff responsible for record keeping | | **Minimum Equipment List (MEL) & Maintenance Programme** | • Issue, update, use and manage the MEL.
• Identify State‑of‑Registry requirements for the maintenance programme and show how the CAMO’s procedure satisfies them.
• Clearly identify sources for the maintenance programme and mandatory tasks. | • CAMO personnel managing MEL and maintenance programme | | **Subcontracting** | • Only subcontract continuing‑airworthiness tasks if the CAMO has procedures for managing those arrangements.
• Contracts must specify that the subcontractor informs the CAMO (and the competent authority) of any changes that affect their ability to fulfil the contract.
• The subcontractor must use procedures that set out how they will meet their responsibilities.
• The CAMO must monitor the subcontractor’s procedures, have access to all relevant data, and retain the authority to override any recommendation if it jeopardises safety.
• The subcontractor must maintain qualified technical expertise and sufficient resources. | • CAMO (contractor)
• Sub‑contracted organisation | **Summary** The CAMO’s responsibilities cover: 1. **Leadership & Authority** – The nominated postholder and the continuing‑airworthiness group must have the authority and resources to manage all tasks. 2. **Human Resources & Training** – Adequate staffing and a clear training policy are required. 3. **Documentation** – A robust records system, accurate flight‑hour/cycle recording, and proper MEL and maintenance programme management. 4. **Subcontracting Controls** – When tasks are outsourced, the CAMO must control the arrangement through contracts, monitoring, and data access, while ensuring the subcontractor remains competent and compliant. These responsibilities ensure that every aircraft under the CAMO’s oversight remains airworthy and compliant with regulatory requirements.
